title: How do I search for products on QuickCrate?
body: QuickCrate offers multiple ways to find products. The search bar at the top of the home screen supports keyword searches — type the product name, brand, or category and tap Search. Results are ranked by relevance and availability. Use filters (left panel) to narrow by category, brand, dietary preference (vegan, gluten-free), price range, or rating. The app also has a voice search feature — tap the mic icon to search hands-free. If you're looking for something specific and can't find it, try alternate spellings or the generic product category. The 'Suggestions' carousel below the search bar shows frequently searched items and trending products in your area. New items added to the catalog appear in the 'New Arrivals' section under each category. If a product is not available, you can request it via 'Suggest a Product' at the bottom of search results.

title: Why is a product showing 'Out of Stock' in my area?
body: A product may show as 'Out of Stock' for several reasons. The most common is that the dark store serving your area has temporarily run out of that item. Stock replenishment happens multiple times a day for high-turnover products, so checking back in a few hours often resolves this. In some cases, the product may be seasonally unavailable (e.g., certain fruits, regional specialties) or may have been removed from our catalog temporarily by the supplier. If the item is available in your app with an 'Out of Stock' label, tap it and enable the 'Notify me when available' toggle — you'll get a push notification as soon as it's restocked. Stock levels vary by zone; an item out of stock in your area may be available in a different part of the city served by a different dark store.

title: Are QuickCrate products authentic and of good quality?
body: Yes, all products on QuickCrate are sourced directly from authorized distributors and brand-authorized wholesalers. We do not sell grey-market or counterfeit goods. Our quality assurance team inspects new supplier relationships and conducts periodic audits of dark-store inventory for freshness, packaging integrity, and expiry compliance. FSSAI-licensed food products are verified for regulatory compliance before onboarding. For branded goods (electronics, personal care, health supplements), we source from brand-authorized channels. If you ever receive a product that appears counterfeit or does not match the listing — including brand name, ingredients, or net weight — report it immediately via 'Report an Issue'. We take such reports seriously and work with suppliers and regulatory bodies when necessary.

title: How does QuickCrate handle product recalls?
body: In the event of a product safety recall (issued by the brand, FSSAI, or another authority), QuickCrate immediately removes the affected product from the catalog in all serviceable zones. If you have recently purchased a recalled product, we proactively send you a recall notification via SMS and in-app alert, along with instructions. In most recall scenarios, you are advised not to consume the product and to initiate a return. QuickCrate processes recall-related returns and refunds with the highest priority — typically within 2 hours of you initiating the return. You do not need to return the physical product for most recalls; we'll issue a full refund and arrange pick-up if necessary. We also publish a recall notice on our in-app 'Safety Notices' page under the Help section.

title: Can I request a product that QuickCrate doesn't currently stock?
body: Yes! We actively encourage customers to suggest products via the 'Suggest a Product' feature. You can find this at the bottom of search results when no matching product is found, or go to Help → Suggest a Product. Fill in the product name, brand, category, and an approximate price if known. Our catalog team reviews all suggestions weekly. Popular requests (products with many suggestions from the same area) are prioritized for procurement. If your suggestion is onboarded, you'll receive a notification and often a small credit as a thank-you. There's no guarantee every suggestion will be added, as availability depends on supplier agreements and cold-chain feasibility for perishables. Your suggestion helps shape QuickCrate's catalog for your community.

title: How accurate are the product images and descriptions on QuickCrate?
body: QuickCrate strives to maintain accurate product images and descriptions that match the physical product you receive. Images are sourced from brands or our photography team and are updated when packaging changes. However, minor discrepancies can occur — for example, a brand may update packaging without changing the product, resulting in a brief lag before our images are updated. Product weights, ingredients, and nutritional information are sourced from the manufacturer and are updated periodically. If you notice a discrepancy between the app listing and the delivered product (e.g., different net weight, changed ingredients), report it via 'Report Listing Issue' on the product detail page. This helps us keep the catalog accurate. Do not rely solely on app listings for allergen information — always check the physical product label.

title: What do the dietary labels on QuickCrate products mean?
body: QuickCrate uses standardized dietary labels on product listings to help you shop according to your preferences. 'Veg' (green dot) and 'Non-Veg' (brown dot) follow FSSAI's mandatory labeling rules for packaged food. 'Organic' indicates the product is certified organic by NPOP or a recognized authority — look for the certification badge. 'Vegan' means the product contains no animal-derived ingredients. 'Gluten-Free' indicates certified gluten-free products suitable for celiac or gluten-intolerant users (though always verify with the physical label for trace allergen warnings). 'Low-Sugar', 'High-Protein', and other nutritional tags are based on manufacturer data. You can filter products by these labels from the category or search results filter panel. Labels are best-effort and QuickCrate is not responsible for undisclosed manufacturer reformulations.

title: How do I check product expiry dates before ordering?
body: QuickCrate displays the 'Best Before' or 'Expiry Date' for applicable products on the product detail page under 'Product Info'. For fresh produce (fruits, vegetables, dairy), instead of a fixed date, we show the number of days remaining from today ('Fresh for X more days'), which is calculated from the lot information scanned at the dark store. This is especially useful for planning weekly groceries. For items with no displayed expiry (cleaning supplies, non-perishable goods), FSSAI does not require expiry dates, or the manufacturer sets a shelf life of 24+ months. If you receive a product with a shorter remaining shelf life than indicated in the app, report it and we'll investigate the inventory management issue. Our system auto-flags products with fewer than 3 days remaining and removes them from sale.

title: Does QuickCrate offer organic or locally sourced products?
body: Yes, QuickCrate has a growing range of organic and locally sourced products. You can browse them under the 'Organic' or 'Farm Fresh' sections on the home screen, or filter by 'Organic' in any product category. Our organic produce is sourced from NPOP-certified farms and is labeled with the certification details on the product page. 'Farm Fresh' products are sourced from local farmers within 200 km of the city's dark stores, reducing transit time and improving freshness. These products are clearly tagged with the farm name or region of origin. Locally sourced availability varies by city — metro areas generally have more options. QuickCrate is committed to expanding its farm-to-door network as part of our sustainability initiative. Seasonal locally sourced items change monthly.

title: Why do product prices sometimes change between visits?
body: Product prices on QuickCrate can change due to several reasons. Suppliers adjust MRPs periodically; these changes are reflected in our app within 24 hours of receiving updated pricing. During sales and promotional events, prices are discounted and revert to standard pricing after the sale ends. Demand-based pricing is not used for any grocery or FMCG product on QuickCrate — we do not surge-price essentials. However, platform fees (delivery, handling) may vary. Price differences between your cart and checkout can sometimes occur if your cart was built during a promotional period and the sale ended before checkout; in such cases, the checkout price is final. You can lock in a sale price by placing the order during the promotion window. For price match queries, contact support within 24 hours of your order.
